---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{r, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(haven)
library(tidyverse)
library(estimatr)
library(sf)
library(modelsummary)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
```

```{r}

# CENSUS DATA 

# Getting people data
Personas_Censo_1992 <- read_sav("C:/Users/mlzam/OneDrive/Documents/repos/chile1988/data/PERSONA.sav")

# Only observations from Chileans who lived in Chile in 1987
Chilenos_Censo_1987 <-Personas_Censo_1992 %>%
  filter(Comuna_1987_Origen3 >= 1101 & Comuna_1987_Origen3 <= 13605, 
         Comuna_1987_Origen3 %% 100 != 0)

# DATA FROM THE PAPER REPLICATION 

# Voting data by county
VotingData_Dataverse <- read_dta("C:/Users/mlzam/OneDrive/Documents/repos/chile1988/data/FinalDatasetForReplication.dta")

# Read shapefile for geometry 
Comunas_Dataverse <- st_read("C:/Users/mlzam/OneDrive/Documents/Files/MIT/spring 26/quant 2/chile1988/dataverse_files (3)/Paper Replication/Data/Map/cl_comunas_geo/Paper Replication/Data/Map/cl_comunas_geo.shp")

# County codes from 1992 census 
Comunas_Censo_1992 <- read_sav("C:/Users/mlzam/OneDrive/Documents/repos/chile1988/data/COMUNAS.sav")

```


```{r}

# Prepping the data 

# First, I am finding each county's numerical ID and attaching it to the 
# county voting data 

# Convert Dataverse voting data to spatial object using lat/lon columns
Dataverse_temp <- VotingData_Dataverse |>
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitud", "latitud"), crs = 4326)

# Use flat geometry instead of spherical to avoid errors
sf_use_s2(FALSE)

# Fix any invalid polygon geometries in the shapefile before joining
Comunas_Dataverse <- st_make_valid(Comunas_Dataverse)

# Spatial join: for each voting data point, find which comuna polygon 
# it falls inside and attach that comuna's ID_2002 and name
crosswalk <- st_join(Dataverse_temp, Comunas_Dataverse |>
  select(ID_2002, NOMBRE)) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>    # drop geometry, no longer needed
  select(comuna, ID_2002, NOMBRE)  # keep only the three columns we need

# Fix NAs manually, then drop unresolvable ones
crosswalk <- crosswalk |>
  mutate(
    ID_2002 = case_when(
      comuna == 53  ~ "1101",  # Iquique
      comuna == 54  ~ "10209", # Quemchi
      comuna == 93  ~ "10101", # Puerto Montt
      comuna == 147 ~ "13403", # Calera de Tango
      comuna == 175 ~ "2102",  # Mejillones
      comuna == 184 ~ "11202", # Cisnes
      comuna == 298 ~ "10210", # Quinchao
      comuna == 87 ~ "5104",  # Juan Fernández
      TRUE ~ ID_2002
    ),
    NOMBRE = case_when(
      comuna == 53  ~ "Iquique",
      comuna == 54  ~ "Quemchi",
      comuna == 93  ~ "Puerto Montt",
      comuna == 147 ~ "Calera de Tango",
      comuna == 175 ~ "Mejillones",
      comuna == 184 ~ "Cisnes",
      comuna == 298 ~ "Quinchao",
      comuna == 87 ~ "Juan Fernández", 
      TRUE ~ NOMBRE
    )
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(ID_2002))

# Merge county ID into county voting data 
VotingData_Dataverse <- VotingData_Dataverse |>
  left_join(crosswalk, by = "comuna")

```


```{r}
# Sanity check: verify that ID_2002 codes from the shapefile 
# match the Codigo codes in the 1992 census

# Step 1: randomly sample 10 comuna codes from the shapefile
sample_ids <- Comunas_Dataverse |> st_drop_geometry() |> 
  slice_sample(n = 10) |> pull(ID_2002)

# Step 2: look up those 10 codes in the shapefile
# Shows what names the shapefile assigns them
Comunas_Dataverse |> st_drop_geometry() |> 
  filter(ID_2002 %in% sample_ids) |> select(ID_2002, NOMBRE)

# Step 3: look up those same 10 codes in the census
# If names match, the coding systems are the same
Comunas_Censo_1992 |> filter(Codigo %in% sample_ids) |> select(Codigo, Nombre)


# Done with finding the ID from the replication data 

```

```{r}

# Now working on census data 
# First, I must create the county level data from the individual observations 

# Creating the education range variable from people data

# Calculating their years of schooling 

Chilenos_Censo_1987 <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  mutate(years_schooling = case_when(
    Tipo_educacion %in% 0:1  ~ 0,           # Never attended or kinder
    Tipo_educacion == 2      ~ Curso,        # Básica: years 1-8
    Tipo_educacion %in% 3:11 ~ 8 + Curso,   # All media types + Normal
    Tipo_educacion %in% 12:14 ~ 12 + Curso  # CFT, IP, Universidad
  ))

# Placing them in categorical rank variables based on years of schooling 

Chilenos_Censo_1987 <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  mutate(rango = case_when(
    years_schooling >= 13              ~ "high",
    years_schooling >= 9 & years_schooling <= 12 ~ "mid",
    years_schooling <= 8               ~ "low"
  ))


# Creating a bureaucrat dummy variable  

Chilenos_Censo_1987 <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  mutate(burocrata = 
    Rama_actividad %in% c(
      750, 751, 752, 753,  # Public administration and defense
      800, 801, 802, 803,  # Education
      809                  # Adult education and other types
    ) |
    Ocupacion %in% c(
      # Military & police
      110,   # Armed forces, Carabineros, Investigations
      5162,  # Carabineros
      5163,  # Prison guards

      # Executive, legislative, public admin
      1100,  # Members of executive/legislative and public admin
      1110,  # Members of executive and legislative powers
      1120,  # Senior public administration officials
      1130,  # Heads of small communities

      # Legal system
      2421,  # Lawyers
      2422,  # Judges
      2429,  # Legal professionals (except lawyers and judges)
      3432,  # Legal technical assistants
      3439,  # Diplomats/consular officials

      # Education
      2310,  # University/higher education professors
      2320,  # Secondary school teachers
      2330,  # Primary and preschool teachers
      2331,  # Primary school teachers
      2332,  # Preschool teachers
      2340,  # Special education teachers
      2350,  # Other education professionals
      2351,  # Pedagogical methods specialists
      2352,  # General education inspectors
      3310,  # Higher ed teaching assistants and secondary paradocentes
      3311,  # Higher education teaching assistants
      3312,  # Secondary teaching assistants
      3320,  # Primary and preschool teaching assistants
      3321,  # Primary teaching assistants
      3322,  # Preschool teaching assistants
      3330,  # Special education teaching assistants
      3340,  # Technical instructors outside regular education

      # Healthcare
      2221,  # Physicians and surgeons
      2225,  # University nutritionists
      2226,  # University medical technologists
      2227,  # University physiotherapists/kinesiologists
      2231,  # Professional nurses
      2232,  # Professional midwives
      3221,  # Medical practitioners
      3229,  # Speech therapy technicians
      3231,  # Nursing auxiliaries/paramedics
      3232,  # Non-university auxiliary midwives

      # State bureaucrats & inspectors
      3440,  # Public customs and tax administration agents
      3441,  # Customs agents and border inspectors
      3442,  # State treasury officials
      3443,  # State social assistance and pension officials
      3444,  # State licensing and permits officials
      3449,  # State commercial establishment inspectors
      3450,  # Police inspectors and detectives

      # Social workers
      2446,  # Professional social workers
      3460   # Technical social workers and assistants
    )
  )

# Creating the county level variables 

# Proportion of bureaucrats per county
prop_burocrata <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  group_by(Comuna_1987_Origen3) %>%
  summarise(prop_burocrata = as.numeric(mean(burocrata, na.rm = TRUE)))

# Proportion of bureaucrats per rango per comuna
prop_rango <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  group_by(Comuna_1987_Origen3) %>%
  summarise(
    prop_high = as.numeric(mean(burocrata & rango == "high", na.rm = TRUE)),
    prop_mid  = as.numeric(mean(burocrata & rango == "mid",  na.rm = TRUE)),
    prop_low  = as.numeric(mean(burocrata & rango == "low",  na.rm = TRUE))
  )

# Getting population data from closer to the plebiscite 
pop_1987 <- Chilenos_Censo_1987 %>%
  group_by(Comuna_1987_Origen3) %>%
  summarise(pop_1987 = n()) %>%
  mutate(Comuna_1987_Origen3 = as.character(Comuna_1987_Origen3))

# Joining the county level variables to the voting data databases 
VotingData_Dataverse <- VotingData_Dataverse %>%
  left_join(prop_burocrata, by = c("ID_2002" = "Comuna_1987_Origen3")) %>%
  left_join(prop_rango,     by = c("ID_2002" = "Comuna_1987_Origen3")) %>% 
  left_join(pop_1987, by = c("ID_2002" = "Comuna_1987_Origen3"))

```

```{r}

# Getting a sense of the data graphically 

# Distribution of years of schooling
hist(Chilenos_Censo_1987$years_schooling, 
     breaks = 20, 
     main = "Distribution of Years of Schooling",
     xlab = "Years of Schooling")

# Distribution of prop_burocrata across comunas
ggplot(prop_burocrata, aes(x = prop_burocrata)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Bureaucrat Share Across Comunas",
       x = "Proportion of Bureaucrats", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Distribution of prop_high/mid/low across comunas
prop_rango_long <- prop_rango %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(prop_high, prop_mid, prop_low),
               names_to = "rango", values_to = "proportion") %>%
  mutate(rango = recode(rango, 
                        prop_high = "High (13+ years)",
                        prop_mid  = "Mid (9-12 years)", 
                        prop_low  = "Low (≤8 years)"))

ggplot(prop_rango_long, aes(x = proportion, fill = rango)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "white", alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~rango) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Bureaucrat Share by Education Level",
       x = "Proportion", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Average proportion by rank across all counties
prop_rango_long %>%
  group_by(rango) %>%
  summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rango, y = mean_prop, fill = rango)) +
  geom_col(color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Average Bureaucrat Share by Education Level",
       x = "Education Level", y = "Mean Proportion") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# Bureaucrat share vs Vote Share No
VotingData_Dataverse %>%
  left_join(prop_burocrata, by = c("ID_2002" = "Comuna_1987_Origen3")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = prop_burocrata, y = VoteShareNo)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", se = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Bureaucrat Share vs No Vote Share (1988)",
       x = "Proportion of Bureaucrats", y = "Vote Share No") +
  theme_minimal()

```



```{r}

# Rescaling the data for interpretation

VotingData_Dataverse <- VotingData_Dataverse %>%
  mutate(
    prop_burocrata = prop_burocrata * 100,
    prop_high      = prop_high * 100,
    prop_mid       = prop_mid  * 100,
    prop_low       = prop_low  * 100
  )

# Running the regressions 

first_nocontrols <- lm_robust(data = VotingData_Dataverse, 
formula = VoteShareNo ~ prop_burocrata, clusters = IDProv )

first <- lm_robust(data = VotingData_Dataverse, 
formula = VoteShareNo ~  prop_burocrata + DMilitaryPresence + YearPost75 + 
  share_allende70 + lnDistStgo + lnDistRegCapital + pop_1987, clusters = IDProv)

second_nocontrols <- lm_robust(data = VotingData_Dataverse, 
formula = VoteShareNo ~ prop_high + prop_mid, clusters = IDProv )

second <- lm_robust(data = VotingData_Dataverse, 
formula = VoteShareNo ~  prop_high + prop_mid + DMilitaryPresence + YearPost75 + 
  share_allende70 + lnDistStgo + lnDistRegCapital + pop_1987, clusters = IDProv)

```

```{r}

tidy_model <- function(model, model_name, keep_terms) {
  as.data.frame(summary(model)$coefficients) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("term") %>%
    rename(estimate = Estimate, se = `Std. Error`, ci_low = `CI Lower`, ci_high = `CI Upper`) %>%
    filter(term %in% keep_terms) %>%
    mutate(model = model_name)
}

# Plot 1: prop_burocrata with and without controls
bind_rows(
  tidy_model(first_nocontrols, "No Controls", "prop_burocrata"),
  tidy_model(first,            "With Controls", "prop_burocrata")
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
                 height = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Bureaucrat Share vs Vote Share No",
       x = "Estimate (95% CI)", y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Plot 2: prop_high + prop_mid with and without controls
bind_rows(
  tidy_model(second_nocontrols, "No Controls",   c("prop_high", "prop_mid")),
  tidy_model(second,            "With Controls", c("prop_high", "prop_mid"))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = term, color = model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
                 height = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Bureaucrat Rank vs Vote Share No",
       x = "Estimate (95% CI)", y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

```{r}
# 
tidy_model <- function(model, model_name, keep_terms) {
  as.data.frame(summary(model)$coefficients) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("term") %>%
    rename(estimate = Estimate, se = `Std. Error`, ci_low = `CI Lower`, ci_high = `CI Upper`) %>%
    filter(term %in% keep_terms) %>%
    mutate(model = model_name)
}

controls <- c("DMilitaryPresence", "YearPost75", "share_allende70", 
              "lnDistStgo", "lnDistRegCapital", "pop_1987")

# Plot 1: prop_burocrata
bind_rows(
  tidy_model(first_nocontrols, "No Controls",   "prop_burocrata"),
  tidy_model(first,            "With Controls", c("prop_burocrata", controls))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate), color = model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
                 height = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Model 1: Bureaucrat Share vs Vote Share No",
       x = "Estimate (95% CI)", y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Plot 2: prop_high + prop_mid
bind_rows(
  tidy_model(second_nocontrols, "No Controls",   c("prop_high", "prop_mid")),
  tidy_model(second,            "With Controls", c("prop_high", "prop_mid", controls))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(term, estimate), color = model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
                 height = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Model 2: Bureaucrat Rank vs Vote Share No",
       x = "Estimate (95% CI)", y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}

modelsummary(
  list("(1)" = first_nocontrols, 
       "(2)" = first, 
       "(3)" = second_nocontrols, 
       "(4)" = second),
  coef_map = c(
    "prop_burocrata" = "Prop. Bureaucrats",
    "prop_high"      = "Prop. High rango",
    "prop_low"       = "Prop. Low rango"
  ),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared", "std.errors"), 
    output = "table.png"
)

```
